growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: pasteur.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: pasteur.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic cheese-ripening medium, iron-limited (5 uM EDDHA in coculture / 1.5 uM EDDHA monoculture), casein N source

sec.method: Multi-omics inference: B. aurantiacum synthesizes putrescine, cross-fed to H. alvei; not a measured flux
Dynamic-headspace GC-MS; production increased in coculture

growth_records.cite: Mekuli, Rina et al. Iron limitation promotes metabolic cross-feeding between cheese ripening bacteria. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag100. [PMC13198182]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively only (AUC of growth curves / OD600); no numeric growth rate or doubling time in text
growth_records.snippet: For B. aurantiacum, casein was used as a nitrogen source because it is able to hydrolyze casein ... B. aurantiacum, which is reported to be an obligate aerobe, and siderophore production is strain-dependent
growth_records.notes: Full medium composition + conditions stated (25 C, pH 7, 200 rpm shake flask, batch). B. aurantiacum 8(6) does NOT produce siderophore (CAS negative at 1.5 uM EDDHA). No numeric growth rate/doubling time or quantitative flux reported (AUC of OD600 only). Putrescine secretion and VSC production are qualitative/omics-inferred, not measured rates. Record retained for medium+condition value.
